repository.name: move-anything-waveform-editor
repository.description: Waveform Editor tool for Move Everything - trim, gain adjust, normalize, and edit WAV files on the Ableton Move

commit.message:
Refactor DSP to stereo, fix drum preset export, and misc improvements

- Refactor DSP from mono int16 to interleaved stereo int16 (L,R,L,R,...)
- Mono WAV files duplicated to both channels on load
- Support PCM 16-bit, PCM 24-bit, and IEEE float 32-bit input
- WAV output always stereo 16-bit PCM
- Waveform display and zero-crossing use L channel
- Fix DSP export symbol: plugin_init -> move_plugin_init_v2
- Fix build.sh to copy from src/dsp/dsp.so (Docker output path)
- Fix drum preset sampleUri: URL-encode spaces (%20)
- Default auto slice threshold from 5 to 25
- Disable loop playback in slice mode, restore on exit
- Back from record-ready state exits module entirely

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

repository.name: move-anything-waveform-editor

commit.message:
Fix recording race condition and improve recorded file handling

- Add "stopping" state to poll sampler finalization before loading WAV,
  preventing "No waveform data" error from async stop
- Show "SAVING..." screen while waiting for WAV to finalize
- Track recorded files as unsaved (dirty) with discard-on-exit cleanup
- Add timestamp to edit filenames (sample_edit_0306_1423.wav)
- Clean up recording UI header (remove debug VU display)
- Add dirty param support to DSP plugin

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Remap knobs: E4=vertical scale, E5=gain/threshold, E7/E8=twist actions

- E3: zoom only (removed shift+E3 vertical scale)
- E4: vertical scale for all modes (was shift+E3)
- E5: gain/normalize in trim/loop, threshold in slice auto (was E4)
- E7 touch+twist: toggle loop on/off
- E8 touch+twist: snap to zero crossing
- Twist prompts stay visible while knob is touched
- Add snapToZeroCrossing() helper function
- Recording waveform: scrolling write-head visualization
- Recording stop: poll for sampler finalization before loading
- Shift+pad preview: 5% of selection, clamped 0.25s-5s
- Update help.json for new knob layout

Each phase now writes a timestamped progress line to the work shard via

into work progress without attaching to tmux.

feat(forms): add server-driven forms with fromSchema() (#7)

* feat(forms): add server-driven forms with fromSchema()

Enable backends to send form definitions as JSON that the frontend
hydrates into fully functional forms. Includes schema validation,
validation descriptor resolution, effect handler currying, repeatable
support, and comprehensive error reporting via SchemaValidationError.

Key fixes included:
- Invalid regex patterns now throw SchemaValidationError (not SyntaxError)
- Negative min/max values on repeatables are rejected during validation

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor(forms): remove arbitrary 3-field-per-row limit

The limit was a surface-level builder guard — neither the type system
(FormFieldRow.fields: FormFieldConfig[]) nor the renderer (FormRow.tsx)
imposed it. Removing it fixes a silent bug in fromSchema() where >3
fields were auto-split into separate rows without matching the schema.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* test(forms): add comprehensive e2e tests for fromSchema()

30 tests covering rendering, validation, conditions, effects,
repeatables, submission, and a full integration signup form scenario.

fix: 修復 TypeError: Cannot set properties of null (v1.1.2)

根因：chrome.tabs.query({ active: true, currentWindow: true }) 可能回傳
空陣列，導致 activeTab 為 undefined，初始化中斷，後續 DOM 操作
對 null 元素設定 textContent 而報錯。

修復：
- 初始化加上 try/catch 與多重 fallback（activeTab → lastFocused window）
- getElementById 結果全部加 null check
- refreshOpenGroups / refreshClosedGroups 加 try/catch
- showToast 加 null check

fix(bol-sync): resolve competitor analysis timeout by deprecating extended sync

Problem: Extended sync (syncType='extended') was executing competitor analysis
synchronously within bol-sync-trigger.ts, exceeding Vercel's 10-minute timeout.

Solution:
- Removed 140 lines of synchronous competitor code from extended case
- Replaced with deprecation notice directing to 'competitor' sync type
- Competitor sync already uses dedicated endpoint (bol-sync-competitor-analysis.ts)
- Updated frontend to remove "Extended Data" button, renumber phases
- Removed 'extended' from BolSyncType definition

Changes:
- api/bol-sync-trigger.ts: Deprecated 'extended', updated JSDoc
- src/lib/bol-api.ts: Removed 'extended' from BolSyncType
- src/pages/BolDashboard.tsx: Removed extended from UI, state, and runAllPhases()

Result: No more timeouts. Competitor analysis runs via dedicated endpoint
with proper timeout handling. TypeScript clean (npm run typecheck passes).

commit.message:
fix: move simulation log emission to fire-and-forget background tasks

emit_logs() now schedules log streaming as an asyncio background task
instead of blocking the HTTP response. Previously, a simulation with
30 log entries at 2s delay would hold the connection for 60+ seconds,
often exceeding browser timeout limits. Now the response returns
immediately while logs continue streaming via Socket.io.

commit.message:
Remove Android-specific check from stopAudioElement method (#60)

## Summary
Removed the Android-specific conditional check (`!this.isAndroid`) from
the `stopAudioElement()` method to allow audio element pause
functionality on all platforms.

## Key Changes
- Removed the `!this.isAndroid` condition from the media-element output
mode check
- Simplified the conditional logic to only check for `outputMode ===
"media-element"` and `audioElement` existence
- Updated the comment to reflect that the no-op now only applies to
direct mode (removed Android reference)

## Implementation Details
The method now consistently pauses the audio element when in
media-element mode, regardless of the platform. This change suggests
that the previous Android-specific workaround is no longer necessary or
was overly restrictive.

https://claude.ai/code/session_017EdwF1TKc2aQGvQ5mzzvKd

fix: province dropdown shows only provinces with future events (#12)

* fix: derive province dropdown from future events only

Previously the province list was built from all loaded events,
while the displayed events are filtered to date >= today.
A province with only past events (not yet deleted by the scraper)
would appear in the dropdown but yield zero results when selected.
Now both the dropdown and the event list use the same date boundary.

already added via migration). Frontend reads province_name from the
JOIN and uses it for display in the filter dropdown, while keeping
the province code for filtering logic.

- viewTracker/voteTracker: rewritten to use @vercel/kv (Redis) with in-memory fallback
- inventory: use Steam Web API directly with STEAM_API_KEY (no IP restrictions)
- skinport-prices: new Edge proxy route (handles Brotli natively)
- siteBase(): prefer NEXT_PUBLIC_APP_URL over VERCEL_URL (avoids deployment protection 401)
- inventory route: treat HTTP 400 as private inventory
- All tracker functions now async, call sites updated

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix claim verification: FRED-first approach for macro claims

Restructure the query generation prompt to lead with FRED API instead of
treating it as an afterthought after DuckDB. Mortgage rates (MORTGAGE30US),
GDP, unemployment, ISM, CPI etc. should always be verified via FRED — never
marked "data not available." Also fix the rewrite prompt that was biased
toward Zillow/Redfin precomputed stats.

commit.message:
Add MinIO and enable Next.js image optimization (#59)

* Add MinIO for local S3 storage and enable Next.js image optimization

Replace local filesystem uploads with MinIO (S3-compatible) in Docker Compose
so local dev matches production's S3 storage path. Enable Next.js image
optimization by configuring remotePatterns + dangerouslyAllowLocalIP for dev.
Change API's /uploads/:key from redirect to proxy so the optimizer can fetch
image bytes directly.

- Add MinIO + minio-init to docker-compose.yml and devcontainer
- Switch default storage provider to S3 with MinIO credentials
- Proxy S3 objects instead of redirecting to signed URLs
- Add S3StorageService.getObject() for streaming
- Rename db:up/down/restart scripts to docker:up/down/restart
- Update CLAUDE.md and setup.sh for MinIO

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix review issues: stream type, null guard, docs, and restart safety

- Replace WebStream with NodeJS.ReadableStream for Fastify compatibility
- Add null guard for S3 response body instead of non-null assertion
- Clarify .env.example credentials are MinIO dev defaults
- Add MinIO ports to CLAUDE.md port assignments
- Make docker:restart use `up -d` so minio-init recreates bucket if needed
- Add production bucket policy note in docker-compose

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix ESLint no-undef error for NodeJS namespace in storage service

Use GetObjectCommandOutput type from AWS SDK instead of NodeJS.ReadableStream
to avoid the no-undef lint error while keeping correct types.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix flaky E2E: increase trip update assertion timeout for WebKit CI

The OPTIMISTIC_TIMEOUT (1s) was too tight for iPhone/WebKit on CI runners.
The h1 update after trip mutation can take longer than 1s in slow CI
environments. Switched to ELEMENT_TIMEOUT (10s) and added 1 retry in CI.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Revert "Fix flaky E2E: increase trip update assertion timeout for WebKit CI"

This reverts commit 75aa4ad6a47f82bf0c736c483c549c47f94b87ac.

* Fix flaky E2E: increase trip update assertion timeout for WebKit CI

OPTIMISTIC_TIMEOUT (1s) was too tight for iPhone/WebKit on CI runners.
Switched to ELEMENT_TIMEOUT (10s) for the h1 update check after mutation.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: make verbose logging a session-only setting that resets on restart (#157)

Verbose logging can expose sensitive data (auth tokens, cookies, API
responses, MusicKit credentials). As a safety measure, it now always
resets to off on app startup — users must re-enable it each session.

- Reset verbose_activity_log to false in load_settings() on startup
- Add session-only note to toggle description and warning box in UI
- Update settings.rs doc comment documenting session-only behavior
- Update help/troubleshooting.md with session-only callout

Closes #157
